summary.intent: Fix 6 P2 contract bugs in the image_cropper component identified by a Codex review, using TDD: write failing tests first, then implement fixes, then sync the spec.
summary.outcome: All 6 findings addressed (8 new tests written first, all passing after fixes), proptest strengthened with rotation-range invariant, spec synced and validated, xci-fast gate passed, changes committed and pushed.

repo: The project uses a spec-code contract: changes to the Rust implementation must be mirrored in markdown spec files under spec/components/specialized/
A `cargo xci-fast` gate must pass before pushing; it runs clippy + full test suite (5264+ tests) including proptests
Codex is used as a code review tool that posts GitHub PR review threads; findings are fetched via GitHub API and resolved inline
The state machine uses a `Bindable` abstraction: `SyncCrop` fires only in controlled mode where `Bindable::get()` returns the parent's authoritative value, making enforcement there inert
MIN_CROP_SIZE = 0.05 is used as the floor for crop dimensions, referenced as a named const in both code and spec

code.finding: New shared helpers `constrain_to_ratio` and `normalize_rotation` were extracted; `constrain_to_ratio` floors both dimensions at max(MIN_CROP_SIZE, MIN_CROP_SIZE * ratio) to preserve aspect ratio under lock
Init arm now clamps zoom to min_zoom..=max_zoom and runs enforce_aspect_ratio on the initial crop rect
Reset arm now restores props.default_crop + clamped initial zoom + flip, not hardcoded defaults
Both keydown handlers guard with early-return on data.is_composing to prevent IME composition sequences from triggering keyboard shortcuts
SetRotation uses normalize_rotation to wrap into [-180, 180] rather than clamp; SetCropArea enforces aspect ratio after area is set
Proptest strengthened with rotation-range invariant asserting rotation stays in [-180.0, 180.0] after any SetRotation sequence

workflow: TDD flow enforced: write failing tests first (confirmed 8 fail), then implement, then verify all pass
Codex review threads are fetched via Bash/GitHub API and addressed as a batch; inline replies note judgment calls (e.g., wrap vs clamp, SyncCrop exclusion)
Spec sync is a required step after implementation changes; a snippet-compile + snapshot-orphan validation gate exists for the spec
Background tasks (xci-fast, Codex polling) are used to parallelize waiting and implementation work

friction: SyncCrop enforce was initially added then reverted after recognizing it would be inert in controlled mode — required a re-read of the Bindable abstraction to catch
The resize aspect block in the spec used 0.05 literals inconsistently with the MIN_CROP_SIZE const, requiring a separate pass to add the const and replace literals
open_items: Inline PR thread replies and resolutions for all 6 Codex findings are pending (noted as next step after gate passed)
Re-triggering Codex after the fixes are pushed to verify no new findings

IME test send closures are intentionally never invoked in tests (proving no event fires during composition) — this leaves those lines as known uncovered, which may need a comment or suppression annotation
